results.rendered_html:
murāri (mura + ari) m.
1〉 Muraʼs Feind, Bein. Kṛṣṇaʼs oder Viṣṇuʼs  VOP. 2,1 .  H. 221 , Sch.  HALĀY. 1,21 .  Verz. d. Oxf. H. 185,a,3 .  KATHĀS. 18,21 .  Spr. 543 .  GĪT. 1,37 .  BHĀG. P. 3,7,14 .  PAÑCAR. 4,1,29 .
— 2〉 N. pr. des Verfassers des Murārināṭaka oder Anargharāghava  Verz. d. B. H. No. 550. fg.   Verz. d. Oxf. H. 137,a, No. 263 . 124,b,7 . 163,a,8 .  Spr. 1239 .  UJJVAL. _zu_ UṆĀDIS. 4,171 . 5,19 .  SIDDH. K. _zu_ P. 3,2,26 . N. pr. eines Scholiasten der Kātantra-Grammatik  COLEBR. Misc. Ess. II, 45 .

results.rendered_html: murāri Author of the Anargha-rāghava mentioned by the poet Ratnākara (who flourished in the 9th century) in Haravijaya 38. 67. He must, therefore be placed before the 9th century.
